OpenFHE-Numpy Documentation

Welcome to OpenFHE-Numpy! This library provides NumPy-compatible matrix operations that can be performed on encrypted data using homomorphic encryption.

OpenFHE-Numpy allows you to perform mathematical operations on encrypted data without decrypting it, maintaining privacy while enabling computation on sensitive information.

Contents:

  • Installation
    • Prerequisites
    • Installing OpenFHE
    • Installing OpenFHE-Numpy
    • Verification
    • Development Installation

    • Formatting
    • Matrix Library
    • Packing

Features

  • NumPy Compatibility: Familiar NumPy-like API for encrypted computations

  • Homomorphic Encryption: Perform operations on encrypted data

  • Matrix Operations: Support for matrix arithmetic, linear algebra, and reductions

  • Type Safety: Full type hints and comprehensive documentation

Quick Example

import numpy as np
import openfhe_numpy as onp

# Create encrypted matrices
a = onp.array([[1, 2], [3, 4]], encrypted=True)
b = onp.array([[5, 6], [7, 8]], encrypted=True)

# Perform encrypted operations
c = onp.add(a, b)       # Element-wise addition
d = onp.matmul(a, b)    # Matrix multiplication
e = onp.sum(a, axis=0)  # Sum along axis
